revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Eta Anakannon Kofun]][[ja:江田穴観音古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Eta Anakannon Kofun | native_name =江田穴観音古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Eta Anakannon Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Eta Anakannon Kofun | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Eta Anakannon Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.97676249|N|130.60586675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nagomi|qid=Q1156415|1=Nagomi, Kumamoto}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|58|36|N|130|36|21|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Eta Anakannon kofun'''|江田穴観音古墳}} is a late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Eta neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Nagomi, Kumamoto|en|Nagomi, Kumamoto|qid=Q1156415|lt=Nagomi|1=Nagomi, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211530|title=江田穴観音古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> The name of the tomb comes from the fact that a statue of [[:en:Kannon Bosatsu|Kannon Bosatsu]] was enshrined in the passageway. ==Overview== The Eta Anakannon Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17 meters and a height of approximately 3.5 meters, with a seven-meter diameter flattened surface on the top. It is situated at an elevation of approximately 27 meters at the southern end of the Suwahara Plateau on the left bank of the downstream Kikuchi River and is located on the slope of the plateau, so its exact size is unclear. Theb tumulus contains a multi-chambered horizontal-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] consisting of a front chamber and a back chamber, with the entrance facing south-southeast. All the stone materials are monoliths of cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} originating from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|qid=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}}. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found in [[archaeological excavation]]s include gold rings, glass ''[[magatama]]'', gilt bronze apricot leaves which were part of a horse harness, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, from which the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Because fires have been lit in the rear chamber for many years, both the rear chamber and the front chamber are covered in soot, making it difficult to see the decorations on the stone chamber walls, but decorative patterns made with red pigments have been found in the passageway, indicating that this was originally a [[decorated kofun]]. Traces of a decorative border remain on the front of the second entrance gate (antechamber).The tumulus was constructed for multiple burials. In the rear chamber, three platforms for coffins are arranged in a U-shape, and in the front chamber, two platforms were located to the left and right.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The Nagomi Town History and Folklore Museum is located nearby.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links==
{{Kofun navbar}}
}}
